question: Does the held-out-gated fine-tune beat stock eng across fonts AND survive real-scan-style degradation, not just the single clean OCR-B cell?

recipe.eval_blur: the 40 held-out eval lines rendered then Gaussian-blurred (sigma 0.8) as a scanner/camera proxy; re-saved as uncompressed grayscale TIFF because text2image emits G4 bilevel that cannot hold a blurred image
recipe.gate: lstmeval CER on withheld eval sets for both stock eng and the fine-tune

verdict: ACCEPT (measured), unanimous. Held-out-gated fine-tuning drives CER to 0.000% on every font tested, on BOTH clean and blurred held-out sets, while stock eng ranges 0.8-4.6%. The 2026-07-18 corrected recipe generalizes across fonts and survives real-scan-style blur. Phase 3 (stock measurement) and Phase 4 (train-beats-stock gate) are closed on data.
caveats: 3 fonts × 1 alphabet (base16c); a base64/ascii alphabet sweep was not run (base16c is the shipped channel).
Blur (sigma 0.8) is a synthetic scanner proxy, not a genuine print/scan; end-to-end real-scan restore is guarded separately by the physical_scan_regression_gate draft.
40 held-out lines per cell — a strong but small eval set.
VM diagnostics only; any shipped accuracy claim must come from CI.
These trained models are NOT cleared for distribution — that is a Phase 5 licensing decision (OFL Reserved-Font-Name + model-data), independent of accuracy.
